@@ -87,8 +87,22 @@ Current main already supports the requested Docker/headless-node workflow: run t
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing from a Codex review: current main already supports the containerized/headless node flow, but the supported path is `openclaw node run`, not `openclaw node install`. The CLI separates those explicitly: `node run` is the foreground path, while `node install` is the service-manager path (`src/cli/node-cli/register.ts:41`, `src/cli/node-cli/register.ts:75`, `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts:89`). The node host connects as a normal paired device with `role: "node"` (`src/node-host/runner.ts:179`), so `pairing required` on first connect is expected until you approve it with `openclaw devices list` and `openclaw devices approve <requestId>` (`docs/cli/node.md:109`, `docs/nodes/index.md:23`, `docs/cli/devices.md:51`). This same behavior is already present in release `v2026.4.22`, so there is not an open core feature gap left to track here.
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` already supports the containerized/headless node flow. The supported command is `openclaw node run`; `openclaw node install` is the service-manager path, so it is not the right entrypoint for a foreground container process.
+
+What I checked:
+- `src/cli/node-cli/register.ts:41` registers `node run` as the foreground node-host command.
+- `src/cli/node-cli/register.ts:75` registers `node install` separately for service installation.
+- `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts:89` backs the service-manager install path.
+- `src/node-host/runner.ts:179` connects the node host as a normal paired device with `role: "node"`.
+- `docs/cli/node.md:109` and `docs/nodes/index.md:23` document the first-run pairing flow: list the pending request, then approve it with `openclaw devices approve <requestId>`.
+- The same behavior is already present in `v2026.4.22`, so this is not an open core feature gap on current releases.
+
+The confusing part is understandable: `pairing required` is expected on first connect until the node device is approved. For container/headless use, `openclaw node run` plus device approval is the supported path.
+
+Codex evidence: reviewed against `cec34821754a`; fixed in `v2026.4.22`.
+
